key_insight: The paper replaces expensive eigenvalue-based adaptive coarse-space construction in overlapping Schwarz methods with transferable neural networks that predict both the coarse basis functions and how many are needed.
engineering_relevance: This can substantially reduce setup cost while preserving robustness for large, high-contrast linear and nonlinear PDE solvers in parallel computing workflows.
method: Two-stage ML pipeline: regression networks predict adaptive coarse basis functions.
Classification network predicts the required number of basis functions for robustness.
Uses sign-invariant losses and post-processing to improve predicted constraints; transfers to elasticity and p-Laplace without retraining.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports testing first on high-contrast scalar diffusion and then transferring the learned model, without retraining, to linear elasticity and nonlinear p-Laplace within a nonlinear Schwarz framework.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of predicted constraints, so incorrect basis counts or sign ambiguities could still affect solver robustness in unseen regimes.
paper_title: Learning Adaptive Coarse Spaces Using Transferable Neural Network Models for Linear and Nonlinear Overlapping Domain Decomposition Methods
